name: twiml
context: TwiML: Twilio's XML markup language for controlling voice calls and messaging responses
info: TwiML (Twilio Markup Language) is an XML-based language that instructs Twilio how to handle voice calls and incoming messages. When Twilio receives a call or message, it requests TwiML from your application's webhook URL and executes the instructions. Voice TwiML verbs include Say (text-to-speech), Play (audio files), Gather (DTMF and speech input), Record (audio recording), Dial (call transfer and conferencing), and Redirect (flow control). Messaging TwiML verbs include Message (send a reply) and Redirect. TwiML Bins provide hosted TwiML snippets for simple use cases without running a server.
body:
# TwiML (Twilio Markup Language)

TwiML is Twilio's XML-based instruction set for controlling voice calls and messaging. When a call or message arrives at your Twilio number, Twilio makes an HTTP request to your webhook and executes the TwiML you return.

## Voice Verbs

Voice TwiML controls what happens during a phone call:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Response>
  <Say voice="alice">Welcome to our service.</Say>
  <Gather numDigits="1" action="/menu">
    <Say>Press 1 for sales. Press 2 for support.</Say>
  </Gather>
  <Say>We didn't receive any input. Goodbye.</Say>
</Response>
```

| Verb | Purpose |
|------|----------|
| `<Say>` | Text-to-speech — reads text aloud |
| `<Play>` | Play an audio file (MP3, WAV) |
| `<Gather>` | Collect DTMF digits or speech input |
| `<Record>` | Record audio from the caller |
| `<Dial>` | Connect to another number, SIP, or conference |
| `<Redirect>` | Redirect to a different TwiML URL |
| `<Pause>` | Wait for a specified number of seconds |
| `<Hangup>` | End the call |
| `<Reject>` | Reject an incoming call |
| `<Enqueue>` | Place the caller in a queue |

## Messaging Verbs

Messaging TwiML controls replies to inbound SMS and WhatsApp messages:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Response>
  <Message>Thanks for your message! We'll get back to you soon.</Message>
</Response>
```

| Verb | Purpose |
|------|----------|
| `<Message>` | Send a reply message (text and/or media) |
| `<Redirect>` | Redirect to a different TwiML URL |

Send media in a reply:

```xml
<Response>
  <Message>
    <Body>Here is your receipt</Body>
    <Media>https://example.com/receipt.pdf</Media>
  </Message>
</Response>
```

## Gathering Input

The `<Gather>` verb collects DTMF (keypad) or speech input during a call:

```javascript
const { VoiceResponse } = require("twilio").twiml;

const twiml = new VoiceResponse();
const gather = twiml.gather({
  numDigits: 1,
  action: "/handle-input",
  method: "POST",
  input: "dtmf speech",
  speechTimeout: "auto",
});
gather.say("Press 1 or say sales for sales. Press 2 or say support for support.");
twiml.say("No input received. Goodbye.");
```

When the user provides input, Twilio POSTs to the `action` URL with `Digits` (DTMF) or `SpeechResult` (speech) parameters.

## Say and Play

The `<Say>` verb converts text to speech with configurable voice and language:

```xml
<Say voice="Polly.Joanna" language="en-US">Hello, how can I help you today?</Say>
```

Available voices: `alice` (default), `man`, `woman`, and Amazon Polly voices (`Polly.Joanna`, `Polly.Matthew`, etc.). The `<Play>` verb streams an audio file:

```xml
<Play>https://example.com/hold-music.mp3</Play>
```

## TwiML Bins

TwiML Bins are Twilio-hosted TwiML snippets for simple use cases. Create them in the Console (Runtime > TwiML Bins) and point your phone number's webhook to the Bin URL. Bins support handlebars-style template variables like `{{From}}` and `{{To}}`:

```xml
<Response>
  <Say>Hello caller from {{From}}. You reached {{To}}.</Say>
</Response>
```

Bins are ideal for static responses, prototyping, and simple IVR flows without a server.

## Programmatic TwiML

Use the Twilio helper library to build TwiML in code:

```javascript
const { VoiceResponse, MessagingResponse } = require("twilio").twiml;

// Voice
const voiceTwiml = new VoiceResponse();
voiceTwiml.say({ voice: "alice" }, "Hello!");
voiceTwiml.play("https://example.com/audio.mp3");
console.log(voiceTwiml.toString()); // XML string

// Messaging
const msgTwiml = new MessagingResponse();
msgTwiml.message("Got your message!");
console.log(msgTwiml.toString()); // XML string
```

sections.title: Voice TwiML verbs
sections.body: Voice verbs control call flow: `<Say>` for text-to-speech, `<Play>` for audio files, `<Gather>` for DTMF/speech input, `<Record>` for recording, `<Dial>` for transfers and conferencing, `<Redirect>` for flow control, `<Pause>` for delays, `<Hangup>` to end, `<Reject>` to decline, `<Enqueue>` for queuing. Verbs execute sequentially. Nest `<Say>` or `<Play>` inside `<Gather>` to prompt while collecting input. See [[twilio/voice#twiml-response]] for server-side examples.

sections.title: Messaging TwiML verbs
sections.body: Messaging verbs: `<Message>` sends a reply with optional `<Body>` and `<Media>` child elements, `<Redirect>` fetches TwiML from another URL. Use messaging TwiML in webhook responses for inbound SMS (see [[twilio/sms#receive]]) and WhatsApp messages (see [[twilio/whatsapp]]). Multiple `<Message>` elements send multiple replies.

sections.title: Gathering input
sections.body: The `<Gather>` verb collects DTMF digits (`numDigits`, `finishOnKey`) or speech (`input="speech"`, `speechTimeout`). When input is received, Twilio POSTs to the `action` URL with `Digits` or `SpeechResult`. If no input is received before timeout, execution continues to the next verb. Nest `<Say>` or `<Play>` inside `<Gather>` to prompt the caller. See [[twilio/voice#outbound-call]] for IVR examples.

sections.title: Say and Play
sections.body: `<Say>` converts text to speech with configurable `voice` (alice, man, woman, Polly voices), `language`, and `loop`. `<Play>` streams audio from a URL (MP3, WAV). Both can be nested inside `<Gather>`. Use Amazon Polly voices for natural-sounding speech. `<Play digits="ww1928">` sends DTMF tones.

sections.title: TwiML Bins
sections.body: TwiML Bins are Twilio-hosted TwiML documents created in the Console (Runtime > TwiML Bins). They support handlebars variables (`{{From}}`, `{{To}}`, `{{Body}}`) for dynamic content without a server. Assign a Bin URL as the webhook for a phone number. Bins are ideal for prototyping, static responses, and simple flows.
